Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.title: Pepper Bacterial Spot
Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.info: This disease is caused by Xanthomonas bacteria, which creates small black spots on leaves.
steps: Remove and destroy infected leaves immediately
Maintain proper spacing between plants for better airflow
Avoid working with plants when leaves are wet

Pepper__bell___healthy.title: Healthy Pepper Plant
Pepper__bell___healthy.info: Your plant is completely healthy! Continue maintaining it this way.

Potato___Early_blight.title: Potato Early Blight
Potato___Early_blight.info: This is a fungal disease that starts on older leaves and creates circular target-like lesions.
steps: Avoid wetting leaves during irrigation (drip irrigation is best)

Potato___Late_blight.title: Potato Late Blight
Potato___Late_blight.info: This is a highly destructive disease that can devastate an entire field, especially in cool and wet conditions.
steps: Remove infected plants and bury them in the soil

Potato___healthy.title: Healthy Potato Plant
Potato___healthy.info: The potato plant is healthy and growing well. A good harvest is expected!
steps: Keep the soil moist but not waterlogged
Hill up soil around developing potatoes to protect them from sunlight

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.title: Tomato Bacterial Spot
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.info: Small raised spots appear on leaves and fruits.
steps: Keep gardening tools clean and sanitized

Tomato___Early_blight.title: Tomato Early Blight
Tomato___Early_blight.info: A fungal disease that causes brown spots on leaves, leading to yellowing and leaf drop.
steps: Use mulch to prevent soil from splashing onto leaves

Tomato___Late_blight.title: Tomato Late Blight
Tomato___Late_blight.info: This disease spreads rapidly in cool weather and can cause stems to turn black.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.title: Tomato Leaf Mold
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.info: Common in greenhouse-grown tomatoes, appearing as a velvety fungus on the underside of leaves.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.title: Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.info: Small circular spots with gray centers. This disease affects leaves but not fruits.
steps: Rotate tomato planting locations each year

Tomato___Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider.title: Tomato Spider Mites
Tomato___Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider.info: Tiny pests that suck sap from leaves, causing yellowing and white speckling.
steps: Spray a strong stream of water under leaves
Encourage beneficial insects like ladybugs in the garden

Tomato___Target_Spot.title: Tomato Target Spot
Tomato___Target_Spot.info: Large brown lesions develop on leaves, resembling a target pattern.
steps: Grow tomatoes in raised beds or well-drained locations

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.title: Tomato Mosaic Virus
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.info: Leaves become small, distorted, and develop a light and dark green mosaic pattern.
steps: Avoid smoking near tomato plants as the virus can spread through tobacco products
products: Protect plants from aphids and other insect vectors

Tomato___Tomato_YellowLeaf_Curl_Virus.title: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus
Tomato___Tomato_YellowLeaf_Curl_Virus.info: Spread by whiteflies. Leaves curl upward and plant growth becomes stunted.

Tomato___healthy.title: Healthy Tomato Plant
Tomato___healthy.info: Congratulations! Your tomato plant is healthy and thriving.
